items.briefing:
Several cybersecurity vendors are expanding their Australian presence this week. Coro has signed a local distribution deal through Leader, making its security tools more accessible to Australian businesses. Darwinium has also updated its mobile software to better detect scam activity, which is relevant given the ongoing rise in mobile-based fraud targeting Australians. Meanwhile, Rubrik and MEDITECH are partnering to improve cyber resilience for hospitals — an important development as healthcare remains a high-value target for attackers.

For organisations in any sector, it's worth remembering that preparation before an incident matters most. **Tip:** Make sure your team knows who to contact and what steps to follow if a data breach occurs — having a simple, written response plan can significantly reduce the damage.

featured.title: Too Big to Fail – Hyperscale Data Centres Bring New Infrastructure Security Imperatives to Australia
featured.quote: Hyperscale data centres present critical infrastructure security risks for Australia's national interests.
